company or its subsidiary of the complainant’s intention to apply to the Court
under subsection (1) if the directors of the company or its subsidiary do not
bring, diligently prosecute, defend or discontinue the action,

                             (b)    the
complainant is acting in good faith, and

                             (c)    it
appears to be in the interests of the provincial company or its subsidiary that
the action be brought, prosecuted, defended or discontinued.

**(3)**   In connection with an action brought or
intervened in under this section, the Court may at any time make any order it
thinks fit, including, without limiting the generality of the foregoing, any or
all of the following:

                             (a)    an
order authorizing the complainant or any other person to control the conduct of
the action;

                             (b)    an
order giving directions for the conduct of the action;

                             (c)    an
order directing that any amount adjudged payable by a defendant in the action
must be paid, in whole or in part, directly to a former or present security
holder of the provincial company or its subsidiary instead of to the company or
its subsidiary;

                             (d)    an
order requiring the provincial company or its subsidiary to pay reasonable
legal fees incurred by the complainant in connection with the action.

RSA 2000 cI‑3 s775; [2014 c13\\
s49](https://www.canlii.org/en/ab/laws/astat/sa-2014-c-13/latest/sa-2014-c-13.html)

Relief by Court from oppression or
unfairness

776 **(1)**   A
complainant may apply to the Court for an order under this section.

**(2)**   Where, on an application under subsection (1),
the Court is satisfied that in respect of a provincial company or any of its
affiliates

                             (a)    any
act or omission of the company or any of its affiliates effects or threatens to
effect a result,

                             (b)    the
business or affairs of the company or any of its affiliates are, have been or
are threatened to be carried on or conducted in a manner, or

                             (c)    the
powers of the directors of the company or any of its affiliates are, have been
or are threatened to be exercised in a manner,

that is oppressive or unfairly
prejudicial to or that unfairly disregards the interests of any participating
policyholder, security holder or creditor, the Court may make an order to
rectify the matters complained of.

**(3)**   On an application under subsection (1), the
Court may make any interim or final order it considers appropriate, including,
without limitation, any or all of the following orders:

                             (a)    an
order restraining the conduct complained of;

                             (b)    an
order to regulate the company’s affairs by amending its bylaws;

                             (c)    an
order appointing directors in place of or in addition to all or any of the
directors then in office;

                             (d)    an
order directing an issue or exchange of securities;

                             (e)    an
order directing the provincial company to purchase securities of a security
holder;

                              (f)    an
order directing the provincial company or any other person to pay to a security
holder any part of the money paid by the security holder for securities;

                             (g)    an
order directing the company, subject to [section 253(3)](https://www.canlii.org/en/ab/laws/stat/rsa-2000-c-i-3/latest/rsa-2000-c-i-3.html#sec253subsec3_smooth), to pay a dividend to
its participating policyholders or shareholders or a class of its participating
policyholders or shareholders;

                             (h)    an
order varying or setting aside a transaction or contract to which the
provincial company is a party and compensating the company or any other party
to the transaction or contract;

                              (i)    an
order requiring the provincial company, within a time specified by the Court,
to produce to the Court or an interested person financial statements or an
accounting in any other form the Court determines;

                              (j)    an
order compensating an aggrieved person;

                             (k)    an
order directing rectification of the registers or other records of the
provincial company;

                              (l)    an
order requiring the trial of any issue;

                            (m)    an
order for the liquidation and dissolution of the provincial company.

1999 cI‑5.1 s776

Notice of application

777
Where a person other than the [Minister](https://www.canlii.org/en/ab/laws/stat/rsa-2000-c-g-10/latest/rsa-2000-c-g-10.html) makes an application under [section 775](https://www.canlii.org/en/ab/laws/stat/rsa-2000-c-i-3/latest/rsa-2000-c-i-3.html#sec775_smooth)
or [776](https://www.canlii.org/en/ab/laws/stat/rsa-2000-c-i-3/latest/rsa-2000-c-i-3.html#sec776_smooth), that person must give notice of the application to the Minister, and
the Minister may appear and be heard in person or by counsel.

1999 cI‑5.1 s777

Costs

778 **(1)**  A
complainant is not required to give security for costs in any application made
or action brought or intervened in under [section 775](https://www.canlii.org/en/ab/laws/stat/rsa-2000-c-i-3/latest/rsa-2000-c-i-3.html#sec775_smooth) or [776](https://www.canlii.org/en/ab/laws/stat/rsa-2000-c-i-3/latest/rsa-2000-c-i-3.html#sec776_smooth).

**(2)**   In an application made or an action brought or
intervened in under [section 775](https://www.canlii.org/en/ab/laws/stat/rsa-2000-c-i-3/latest/rsa-2000-c-i-3.html#sec775_smooth) or [776](https://www.canlii.org/en/ab/laws/stat/rsa-2000-c-i-3/latest/rsa-2000-c-i-3.html#sec776_smooth), the Court may at any time order the
provincial company or its subsidiary to pay to the complainant interim costs,
including legal fees and disbursements, but the complainant may be held
accountable for the interim costs on final disposition of the application or
action.

1999 cI‑5.1 s778

Stay, etc., of application or
action

779 **(1)**   An
application made or an action brought or intervened in under [section 775](https://www.canlii.org/en/ab/laws/stat/rsa-2000-c-i-3/latest/rsa-2000-c-i-3.html#sec775_smooth) or [776](https://www.canlii.org/en/ab/laws/stat/rsa-2000-c-i-3/latest/rsa-2000-c-i-3.html#sec776_smooth)
must not be stayed or dismissed by reason only that it is shown that an alleged
breach of a right or duty owed to the provincial company or its subsidiary has
been or may be approved by the shareholders of the company or the subsidiary,
but evidence of approval by the shareholders may be taken into account by the
Court in making an order under section 775 or 776.

**(2)**   An application made or an action brought or
intervened in under [section 775](https://www.canlii.org/en/ab/laws/stat/rsa-2000-c-i-3/latest/rsa-2000-c-i-3.html#sec775_smooth) or [776](https://www.canlii.org/en/ab/laws/stat/rsa-2000-c-i-3/latest/rsa-2000-c-i-3.html#sec776_smooth) must not be stayed, discontinued,
settled or dismissed for want of prosecution without the approval of the Court
given on any terms the Court considers appropriate, and if the Court determines
that the interests of any complainant may be substantially affected by the
stay, discontinuance, settlement or dismissal, the Court may order any party to
the application or action to give notice to the complainant.

1999 cI‑5.1 s779

Subpart 2

Offences and Penalties

Offences

780   A person who contravenes any of the
following provisions is guilty of an offence:

                             (a)    in
Part 1,

                                     (i)    in
Subpart 1, [sections 18](https://www.canlii.org/en/ab/laws/stat/rsa-2000-c-i-3/latest/rsa-2000-c-i-3.html#sec18_smooth), [25(2)](https://www.canlii.org/en/ab/laws/stat/rsa-2000-c-i-3/latest/rsa-2000-c-i-3.html#sec25subsec2_smooth), [31(1)](https://www.canlii.org/en/ab/laws/stat/rsa-2000-c-i-3/latest/rsa-2000-c-i-3.html#sec31subsec1_smooth), [36](https://www.canlii.org/en/ab/laws/stat/rsa-2000-c-i-3/latest/rsa-2000-c-i-3.html#sec36_smooth), [39(1)](https://www.canlii.org/en/ab/laws/stat/rsa-2000-c-i-3/latest/rsa-2000-c-i-3.html#sec39subsec1_smooth) and [(5)](https://www.canlii.org/en/ab/laws/stat/rsa-2000-c-i-3/latest/rsa-2000-c-i-3.html#sec39subsec5_smooth), [40](https://www.canlii.org/en/ab/laws/stat/rsa-2000-c-i-3/latest/rsa-2000-c-i-3.html#sec40_smooth), [41](https://www.canlii.org/en/ab/laws/stat/rsa-2000-c-i-3/latest/rsa-2000-c-i-3.html#sec41_smooth), [43(1)](https://www.canlii.org/en/ab/laws/stat/rsa-2000-c-i-3/latest/rsa-2000-c-i-3.html#sec43subsec1_smooth), [(2)](https://www.canlii.org/en/ab/laws/stat/rsa-2000-c-i-3/latest/rsa-2000-c-i-3.html#sec43subsec2_smooth) and
[(4)](https://www.canlii.org/en/ab/laws/stat/rsa-2000-c-i-3/latest/rsa-2000-c-i-3.html#sec43subsec4_smooth), [44(1)](https://www.canlii.org/en/ab/laws/stat/rsa-2000-c-i-3/latest/rsa-2000-c-i-3.html#sec44subsec1_smooth), [45](https://www.canlii.org/en/ab/laws/stat/rsa-2000-c-i-3/latest/rsa-2000-c-i-3.html#sec45_smooth), [46(1)](https://www.canlii.org/en/ab/laws/stat/rsa-2000-c-i-3/latest/rsa-2000-c-i-3.html#sec46subsec1_smooth), [47(1)](https://www.canlii.org/en/ab/laws/stat/rsa-2000-c-i-3/latest/rsa-2000-c-i-3.html#sec47subsec1_smooth), [49](https://www.canlii.org/en/ab/laws/stat/rsa-2000-c-i-3/latest/rsa-2000-c-i-3.html#sec49_smooth), [50](https://www.canlii.org/en/ab/laws/stat/rsa-2000-c-i-3/latest/rsa-2000-c-i-3.html#sec50_smooth) and [56](https://www.canlii.org/en/ab/laws/stat/rsa-2000-c-i-3/latest/rsa-2000-c-i-3.html#sec56_smooth);

                                    (ii)    in
Subpart 2, sections 63, 68(2), 71, 72, 73, 74(1) and 75;

                                   (iii)    in
Subpart 3, [sections 79](https://www.canlii.org/en/ab/laws/stat/rsa-2000-c-i-3/latest/rsa-2000-c-i-3.html#sec79_smooth), [88](https://www.canlii.org/en/ab/laws/stat/rsa-2000-c-i-3/latest/rsa-2000-c-i-3.html#sec88_smooth), [91(1)](https://www.canlii.org/en/ab/laws/stat/rsa-2000-c-i-3/latest/rsa-2000-c-i-3.html#sec91subsec1_smooth), [94(1)](https://www.canlii.org/en/ab/laws/stat/rsa-2000-c-i-3/latest/rsa-2000-c-i-3.html#sec94subsec1_smooth) and [(5)](https://www.canlii.org/en/ab/laws/stat/rsa-2000-c-i-3/latest/rsa-2000-c-i-3.html#sec94subsec5_smooth), [95](https://www.canlii.org/en/ab/laws/stat/rsa-2000-c-i-3/latest/rsa-2000-c-i-3.html#sec95_smooth), [96](https://www.canlii.org/en/ab/laws/stat/rsa-2000-c-i-3/latest/rsa-2000-c-i-3.html#sec96_smooth), [98](https://www.canlii.org/en/ab/laws/stat/rsa-2000-c-i-3/latest/rsa-2000-c-i-3.html#sec98_smooth), [99](https://www.canlii.org/en/ab/laws/stat/rsa-2000-c-i-3/latest/rsa-2000-c-i-3.html#sec99_smooth), [100(1)](https://www.canlii.org/en/ab/laws/stat/rsa-2000-c-i-3/latest/rsa-2000-c-i-3.html#sec100subsec1_smooth), [102](https://www.canlii.org/en/ab/laws/stat/rsa-2000-c-i-3/latest/rsa-2000-c-i-3.html#sec102_smooth),
[103](https://www.canlii.org/en/ab/laws/stat/rsa-2000-c-i-3/latest/rsa-2000-c-i-3.html#sec103_smooth) and [104](https://www.canlii.org/en/ab/laws/stat/rsa-2000-c-i-3/latest/rsa-2000-c-i-3.html#sec104_smooth);

                             (b)    in
Part 2,

                                     (i)    in
Subpart 1, [sections 110(1)](https://www.canlii.org/en/ab/laws/stat/rsa-2000-c-i-3/latest/rsa-2000-c-i-3.html#sec110subsec1_smooth), [112](https://www.canlii.org/en/ab/laws/stat/rsa-2000-c-i-3/latest/rsa-2000-c-i-3.html#sec112_smooth), [113(1)](https://www.canlii.org/en/ab/laws/stat/rsa-2000-c-i-3/latest/rsa-2000-c-i-3.html#sec113subsec1_smooth), [114](https://www.canlii.org/en/ab/laws/stat/rsa-2000-c-i-3/latest/rsa-2000-c-i-3.html#sec114_smooth), [115](https://www.canlii.org/en/ab/laws/stat/rsa-2000-c-i-3/latest/rsa-2000-c-i-3.html#sec115_smooth), [116(1)](https://www.canlii.org/en/ab/laws/stat/rsa-2000-c-i-3/latest/rsa-2000-c-i-3.html#sec116subsec1_smooth), [117](https://www.canlii.org/en/ab/laws/stat/rsa-2000-c-i-3/latest/rsa-2000-c-i-3.html#sec117_smooth) and [118](https://www.canlii.org/en/ab/laws/stat/rsa-2000-c-i-3/latest/rsa-2000-c-i-3.html#sec118_smooth);

[(3)](https://www.canlii.org/en/ab/laws/stat/rsa-2000-c-i-3/latest/rsa-2000-c-i-3.html#sec176subsec3_smooth) and [(5)](https://www.canlii.org/en/ab/laws/stat/rsa-2000-c-i-3/latest/rsa-2000-c-i-3.html#sec176subsec5_smooth), [180(1)](https://www.canlii.org/en/ab/laws/stat/rsa-2000-c-i-3/latest/rsa-2000-c-i-3.html#sec180subsec1_smooth), [187(3)](https://www.canlii.org/en/ab/laws/stat/rsa-2000-c-i-3/latest/rsa-2000-c-i-3.html#sec187subsec3_smooth), [207](https://www.canlii.org/en/ab/laws/stat/rsa-2000-c-i-3/latest/rsa-2000-c-i-3.html#sec207_smooth), [211(1)](https://www.canlii.org/en/ab/laws/stat/rsa-2000-c-i-3/latest/rsa-2000-c-i-3.html#sec211subsec1_smooth) and [213](https://www.canlii.org/en/ab/laws/stat/rsa-2000-c-i-3/latest/rsa-2000-c-i-3.html#sec213_smooth);

                                   (iii)    in
Subpart 3, [sections 216](https://www.canlii.org/en/ab/laws/stat/rsa-2000-c-i-3/latest/rsa-2000-c-i-3.html#sec216_smooth), [217(1)](https://www.canlii.org/en/ab/laws/stat/rsa-2000-c-i-3/latest/rsa-2000-c-i-3.html#sec217subsec1_smooth) and [(2)](https://www.canlii.org/en/ab/laws/stat/rsa-2000-c-i-3/latest/rsa-2000-c-i-3.html#sec217subsec2_smooth), [218(1)](https://www.canlii.org/en/ab/laws/stat/rsa-2000-c-i-3/latest/rsa-2000-c-i-3.html#sec218subsec1_smooth), [219(1)](https://www.canlii.org/en/ab/laws/stat/rsa-2000-c-i-3/latest/rsa-2000-c-i-3.html#sec219subsec1_smooth), [220(1)](https://www.canlii.org/en/ab/laws/stat/rsa-2000-c-i-3/latest/rsa-2000-c-i-3.html#sec220subsec1_smooth), [221](https://www.canlii.org/en/ab/laws/stat/rsa-2000-c-i-3/latest/rsa-2000-c-i-3.html#sec221_smooth), [222(3)](https://www.canlii.org/en/ab/laws/stat/rsa-2000-c-i-3/latest/rsa-2000-c-i-3.html#sec222subsec3_smooth)
and [(6)](https://www.canlii.org/en/ab/laws/stat/rsa-2000-c-i-3/latest/rsa-2000-c-i-3.html#sec222subsec6_smooth), [223](https://www.canlii.org/en/ab/laws/stat/rsa-2000-c-i-3/latest/rsa-2000-c-i-3.html#sec223_smooth), [224](https://www.canlii.org/en/ab/laws/stat/rsa-2000-c-i-3/latest/rsa-2000-c-i-3.html#sec224_smooth) and [225](https://www.canlii.org/en/ab/laws/stat/rsa-2000-c-i-3/latest/rsa-2000-c-i-3.html#sec225_smooth);

                                  (iv)    in
